scenario: Hard lockup on CPU with task stuck waiting on qwrlock spinlock
symptoms: Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP
NMI watchdog: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 5

workflow.thought: Check watchdog threshold to understand lockup timing
Examine task stuck on CPU 5 to understand context
workflow.observation: Task stuck in queued_write_lock_slowpath at RIP ffffffff97917812

workflow.thought: Disassemble the lock function to see instruction details
Identify the lock address from task context
Examine lock structure to understand state
workflow.observation: Lock has counter = 0x101 in cnts field

workflow.thought: Check lock's internal spinlock state
Analyze bit pattern of lock value
workflow.observation: Bits set: 24 22 21 19 16 8 0 - lock is locked, pending, contended

workflow.thought: Find other tasks waiting on same lock
workflow.observation: Tasks on CPUs 24, 45, 55, 58 waiting on lock

workflow.thought: Examine internal qrwlock structure for reader/writer state
workflow.observation: wmode = 0x1 (writer waiting), rcnts shows reader count

workflow.thought: Identify function at address from CPU 13's RAX register
Disassemble do_wait function to see lock usage
Examine wait_opts structure for task waiting details
Get address of children list from task structure
List tasks in children list to identify related processes
workflow.observation: Four task addresses in children list

workflow.thought: Show process status for tasks in children list
workflow.observation: ps, grep, grep, awk tasks with different states

workflow.thought: Get backtraces for all tasks in children list
workflow.observation: All tasks in exit paths, awk task stuck in lock

workflow.thought: Check kernel log for rcu_sched stall patterns
workflow.action: log -t | grep rcu_sched | awk '{print $6 " " $7 " " $8}' | sort | uniq -c

workflow.thought: Check kernel log for soft lockup patterns
workflow.action: log -t | grep soft\ lockup | awk '{print $7}' | sort | uniq -c

workflow.thought: Check for blocked task messages in log
List loaded modules to identify third-party drivers
workflow.observation: Mellanox modules mlx_compat, mlx5_core, mlx5_ib loaded

workflow.thought: Check runqueue timestamps for CPU lag

root_cause_analysis: Since the issue went away and never came back after disabling CPU 5, it would be reasonable and proper to believe that the issue was faulty CPU.
